qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unchecked ZIP decompression in columnar upload permits low-privilege denial of service
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is an unchecked ZIP decompression path in the database upload flow, while the advisory is an improper-authorization flaw where an authenticated user bypasses dataset access controls by overwriting the SQL of an existing dataset.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described dataset authorization bypass. The only qualifying finding concerns ZIP decompression in columnar database uploads, which is a different code path, endpoint family, and root-cause mechanism.
evidence: The advisory describes an authenticated user bypassing dataset data-access checks by overwriting the SQL query of an existing dataset.
The only qualifying finding targets POST /api/v1/database/upload_metadata/ and POST /api/v1/database/<pk>/upload/, where ZIP members are fully inflated in ColumnarReader before parsing.
A missing ZIP safety check in upload handling is not equivalent to the advisory's missing or bypassed authorization check on dataset SQL updates.
